paper_title: A New Preparation Method of Microfauna from Gypsum: Micropaleontological Association from the Middle Miocene Badenian Gypsum Deposits of Paratethys

methods.description: A novel method for preparing microfauna samples from gypsum deposits, specifically targeting the micropaleontological associations found in the Middle Miocene Badenian gypsum deposits of Paratethys. This method aims to improve the extraction and preservation of microfauna for further analysis.

steps.description: The gypsum samples are first crushed into smaller pieces to facilitate further processing.
steps.searchable_summary: crush gypsum samples into smaller pieces

steps.description: The crushed gypsum is then dissolved in a weak acid solution to separate the microfauna from the gypsum matrix.
steps.searchable_summary: dissolve crushed gypsum in weak acid to separate microfauna

steps.description: The solution is then filtered to collect the microfauna, which are retained on the filter paper.
steps.searchable_summary: filter solution to collect microfauna on filter paper

steps.description: The collected microfauna are washed with distilled water to remove any remaining gypsum or acid residues.
steps.searchable_summary: wash collected microfauna with distilled water

steps.description: The cleaned microfauna are then dried and prepared for further micropaleontological analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: dry and prepare microfauna for analysis
